4→ 5→# Claude Code Trace 6→ 7→A desktop + web viewer for Claude Code session JSONL files. Built with [Tauri v2](https://v2.tauri.app/) (Rust backend + React frontend). 8→ 9→Reads session logs from `~/.claude/` and renders them as a scrollable conversation with expandable tool calls, token counts, and live tailing. Works as a **native desktop app** (macOS, Linux, Windows) or as a **web app** in any browser. Inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude). 10→ 11→

12→ 13→

14→ 15→## Install 16→ 17→### cargo install (any platform with Rust + Node.js) 18→ 19→```bash 20→git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git 21→cd claude-code-trace 22→./script/install.sh # builds everything + installs to PATH 23→ 24→claude-code-trace # desktop app 25→claude-code-trace --web # web mode (opens browser) 26→cctrace-tui # terminal UI (requires backend running) 27→``` 28→ 29→### Download pre-built 30→ 31→Grab the latest release from [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases): 32→ 33→| Platform | File | 34→| -------- | ------------------------------- | 35→| macOS | `.dmg` | 36→| Linux | `.deb`, `.rpm`, `.AppImage` | 37→| Windows | `.msi`, `.exe` (NSIS installer) | 38→ 39→> [!IMPORTANT] 40→> **macOS:** The app is unsigned. After installing, remove the quarantine attribute: 41→> 42→> ```bash 43→> xattr -cr /Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app 44→> ``` 45→ 46→### Run from source (no install) 47→ 48→```bash 49→git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git 50→cd claude-code-trace 51→npm install 52→npm run tauri dev -- -- --web # opens browser automatically 53→``` 54→ 55→## Requirements 56→ 57→- [Rust](https://rustup.rs/) 1.77+ 58→- Node.js 18+ 59→- macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools (`xcode-select --install`) 60→- Linux: `libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libayatana-appindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev libxdo-dev libssl-dev` 61→- Windows: [WebView2](https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/) (pre-installed on Windows 10/11) 62→ 63→## Usage 64→ 65→### Desktop 66→ 67→Launch the app to open the session picker. It auto-discovers all sessions from `~/.claude/projects/`. 68→ 69→Click **Open in Browser** in the toolbar to switch to browser mode — this opens `http://localhost:1420` in your default browser and hides the desktop window. 70→ 71→### Web (standalone) 72→ 73→If you installed the desktop app: 74→ 75→```bash 76→# macOS 77→/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web 78→ 79→# Or add a shell alias: 80→alias cctrace='/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web' 81→cctrace 82→``` 83→ 84→If running from source: 85→ 86→```bash 87→npm run tauri dev -- -- --web 88→``` 89→ 90→Then open **http://localhost:1420** in any browser. 91→ 92→Select a session to view the conversation. Click messages to expand tool calls, or open the detail view for full inspection. 93→ 94→MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool calls are automatically detected and displayed with human-friendly names. For example, `mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot` renders as **MCP chrome-devtools** with the summary "take screenshot". Supported MCP servers include chrome-devtools, figma, atlassian, buildkite, cloudflare, and any other server following the `mcp____` naming convention. 95→ 96→### Keybindings 97→ 98→`?` toggles keybind hints in any view. 99→ 100→**List view** 101→ 102→| Key | Action | 103→| ----------------- | --------------------------------------- | 104→| `j` / `k` | Move cursor down / up | 105→| `G` / `g` | Jump to last / first message | 106→| `Tab` | Toggle expand/collapse current message | 107→| `e` / `c` | Expand / collapse all Claude messages | 108→| `Enter` | Open detail view | 109→| `d` | Open debug log viewer | 110→| `t` | Open team task board (when teams exist) | 111→| `s` / `q` / `Esc` | Open session picker | 112→ 113→**Detail view** 114→ 115→| Key | Action | 116→| ----------- | ------------ | 117→| `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | 118→ 119→**Session picker** 120→ 121→| Key | Action | 122→| ----------- | --------------------- | 123→| `j` / `k` | Navigate sessions | 124→| `Enter` | Open selected session | 125→| `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | 126→ 127→**Debug log viewer** 128→ 129→| Key | Action | 130→| ----------- | ------------ | 131→| `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | 132→ 133→## TUI (Terminal UI) 134→ 135→A terminal-based viewer that connects to the same HTTP API. No browser needed — just Node.js + a terminal. 136→ 137→```bash 138→# First time setup 139→cd tui && npm install && cd .. 140→ 141→# Single command (starts backend + TUI together): 142→npm run dev:tui 143→ 144→# Or if you installed via script/install.sh: 145→claude-code-trace --web & # start backend in background 146→cctrace-tui # run TUI 147→``` 148→ 149→Features: session picker, message list with expand/collapse, live tailing via SSE, vim-style keybinds (j/k/G/g/Tab/e/c/q). 150→ 151→## Development 152→ 153→```bash 154→npm install 155→npm run tauri dev # desktop app with hot reload 156→npm run dev:web # web mode (opens browser, no desktop window) 157→npm run dev:tui # TUI (starts backend + terminal UI together) 158→npm run tauri build # production build 159→``` 160→ 161→### Check & Test 162→ 163→```bash 164→npm run check # run all checks at once 165→npx vitest run # frontend tests 166→cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust tests 167→npx tsc --noEmit # TypeScript type check 168→npx oxlint # JS/TS lint 169→npx oxfmt # JS/TS format 170→cargo clippy --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust lint 171→cargo fmt --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust format 172→``` 173→ 174→## Release 175→ 176→Push a version tag to trigger a GitHub Actions build: 177→ 178→```bash 179→git tag v0.4.0 180→git push origin v0.4.0 181→``` 182→ 183→This creates a draft release with macOS, Linux, and Windows artifacts attached. Review and publish it from the [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases) page. 184→ 185→## Attribution 186→ 187→Parsing heuristics inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude) and [claude-devtools](https://github.com/matt1398/claude-devtools). 188→ 189→## License 190→ 191→[MIT](LICENSE) 192→

4→ 5→# Claude Code Trace 6→ 7→A desktop + web viewer for Claude Code session JSONL files. Built with [Tauri v2](https://v2.tauri.app/) (Rust backend + React frontend). 8→ 9→Reads session logs from `~/.claude/` and renders them as a scrollable conversation with expandable tool calls, token counts, and live tailing. Works as a **native desktop app** (macOS, Linux, Windows) or as a **web app** in any browser. Inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude). 10→ 11→

12→ 13→

14→ 15→## Install 16→ 17→### cargo install (any platform with Rust + Node.js) 18→ 19→```bash 20→git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git 21→cd claude-code-trace 22→./script/install.sh # builds everything + installs to PATH 23→ 24→claude-code-trace # desktop app 25→claude-code-trace --web # web mode (opens browser) 26→cctrace-tui # terminal UI (requires backend running) 27→``` 28→ 29→### Download pre-built 30→ 31→Grab the latest release from [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases): 32→ 33→| Platform | File | 34→| -------- | ------------------------------- | 35→| macOS | `.dmg` | 36→| Linux | `.deb`, `.rpm`, `.AppImage` | 37→| Windows | `.msi`, `.exe` (NSIS installer) | 38→ 39→> [!IMPORTANT] 40→> **macOS:** The app is unsigned. After installing, remove the quarantine attribute: 41→> 42→> ```bash 43→> xattr -cr /Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app 44→> ``` 45→ 46→### Run from source (no install) 47→ 48→```bash 49→git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git 50→cd claude-code-trace 51→npm install 52→npm run tauri dev -- -- --web # opens browser automatically 53→``` 54→ 55→## Requirements 56→ 57→- [Rust](https://rustup.rs/) 1.77+ 58→- Node.js 18+ 59→- macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools (`xcode-select --install`) 60→- Linux: `libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libayatana-appindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev libxdo-dev libssl-dev` 61→- Windows: [WebView2](https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/) (pre-installed on Windows 10/11) 62→ 63→## Usage 64→ 65→### Desktop 66→ 67→Launch the app to open the session picker. It auto-discovers all sessions from `~/.claude/projects/`. 68→ 69→Click **Open in Browser** in the toolbar to switch to browser mode — this opens `http://localhost:1420` in your default browser and hides the desktop window. 70→ 71→### Web (standalone) 72→ 73→If you installed the desktop app: 74→ 75→```bash 76→# macOS 77→/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web 78→ 79→# Or add a shell alias: 80→alias cctrace='/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web' 81→cctrace 82→``` 83→ 84→If running from source: 85→ 86→```bash 87→npm run tauri dev -- -- --web 88→``` 89→ 90→Then open **http://localhost:1420** in any browser. 91→ 92→Select a session to view the conversation. Click messages to expand tool calls, or open the detail view for full inspection. 93→ 94→MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool calls are automatically detected and displayed with human-friendly names. For example, `mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot` renders as **MCP chrome-devtools** with the summary "take screenshot". Supported MCP servers include chrome-devtools, figma, atlassian, buildkite, cloudflare, and any other server following the `mcp____` naming convention. 95→ 96→### Keybindings 97→ 98→`?` toggles keybind hints in any view. 99→ 100→**List view** 101→ 102→| Key | Action | 103→| ----------------- | --------------------------------------- | 104→| `j` / `k` | Move cursor down / up | 105→| `G` / `g` | Jump to last / first message | 106→| `Tab` | Toggle expand/collapse current message | 107→| `e` / `c` | Expand / collapse all Claude messages | 108→| `Enter` | Open detail view | 109→| `d` | Open debug log viewer | 110→| `t` | Open team task board (when teams exist) | 111→| `s` / `q` / `Esc` | Open session picker | 112→ 113→**Detail view** 114→ 115→| Key | Action | 116→| ----------- | ------------ | 117→| `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | 118→ 119→**Session picker** 120→ 121→| Key | Action | 122→| ----------- | --------------------- | 123→| `j` / `k` | Navigate sessions | 124→| `Enter` | Open selected session | 125→| `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | 126→ 127→**Debug log viewer** 128→ 129→| Key | Action | 130→| ----------- | ------------ | 131→| `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | 132→ 133→## TUI (Terminal UI) 134→ 135→A terminal-based viewer that connects to the same HTTP API. No browser needed — just Node.js + a terminal. 136→ 137→```bash 138→# First time setup 139→cd tui && npm install && cd .. 140→ 141→# Single command (starts backend + TUI together): 142→npm run dev:tui 143→ 144→# Or if you installed via script/install.sh: 145→claude-code-trace --web & # start backend in background 146→cctrace-tui # run TUI 147→``` 148→ 149→Features: session picker, message list with expand/collapse, live tailing via SSE, vim-style keybinds (j/k/G/g/Tab/e/c/q). 150→ 151→## Development 152→ 153→```bash 154→npm install 155→npm run tauri dev # desktop app with hot reload 156→npm run dev:web # web mode (opens browser, no desktop window) 157→npm run dev:tui # TUI (starts backend + terminal UI together) 158→npm run tauri build # production build 159→``` 160→ 161→### Check & Test 162→ 163→```bash 164→npm run check # run all checks at once 165→npx vitest run # frontend tests 166→cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust tests 167→npx tsc --noEmit # TypeScript type check 168→npx oxlint # JS/TS lint 169→npx oxfmt # JS/TS format 170→cargo clippy --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust lint 171→cargo fmt --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust format 172→``` 173→ 174→## Release 175→ 176→Push a version tag to trigger a GitHub Actions build: 177→ 178→```bash 179→git tag v0.4.0 180→git push origin v0.4.0 181→``` 182→ 183→This creates a draft release with macOS, Linux, and Windows artifacts attached. Review and publish it from the [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases) page. 184→ 185→## Attribution 186→ 187→Parsing heuristics inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude) and [claude-devtools](https://github.com/matt1398/claude-devtools). 188→ 189→## License 190→ 191→[MIT](LICENSE) 192→

## Comprehensive Keybinding Audit Report

Based on my thorough search of the codebase, here are all the keybindings found and discrepancies with the README:

### KEYBINDINGS FOUND IN CODE (Web Frontend - src/):

**List View:**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - move down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - move up
- `G` - jump to top (last message)
- `g` - jump to bottom (first message)
- `Tab` - toggle expand current message
- `e` - expand all Claude messages
- `c` - collapse all
- `Enter` - open detail view
- `d` - open debug log viewer
- `t` - open team task board (when teams exist)
- `s` - open session picker
- `q` - open session picker
- `Escape` - open session picker
- `?` - toggle keybind hints
- `h` / `ArrowLeft` - focus sidebar
- No Tab keybinding for list items (only for main messages)

**Detail View:**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - move down in items/panels
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - move up in items/panels
- `Tab` - toggle expand/collapse item
- `Enter` - open subagent or toggle expand
- `h` / `ArrowLeft` - switch to previous column
- `l` / `ArrowRight` - switch to next column
- `q` - back to list
- `Escape` - close rightmost panel or back to list
- `?` - toggle keybind hints

**Session Picker:**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - navigate down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - navigate up
- `Enter` - open selected session
- `q` - back to list
- `Escape` - back to list
- `?` - toggle keybind hints
- `h` / `ArrowLeft` - focus sidebar

**Debug Log Viewer:**
- `q` - back to list
- `Escape` - back to list
- `?` - toggle keybind hints
- `j` / `k` navigation handled via direct keyboard event (onKeyDown in search input)

**Team Board:**
- `q` - back to list
- `Escape` - back to list
- `?` - toggle keybind hints

**Sidebar (all views):**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - navigate down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - navigate up
- `Enter` - select project
- `Escape` - unfocus sidebar
- `l` / `ArrowRight` - unfocus sidebar
- `?` - toggle keybind hints

### KEYBINDINGS FOUND IN CODE (TUI - tui/src/):

**List View:**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - move down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - move up
- `G` - jump to last message
- `g` - jump to first message
- `Tab` - toggle expand
- `Return` - open detail view
- `e` - expand all Claude messages
- `c` - collapse all
- `t` - open team task board
- `d` - open debug log viewer
- `q` / `Escape` - open session picker
- `h` / `ArrowLeft` - focus sidebar

**Detail View (top-level):**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - move down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - move up
- `Tab` - toggle expand
- `Return` - open subagent or toggle expand
- `e` - expand all
- `c` - collapse all
- `q` / `Escape` - back to list

**Detail View (subagent message list):**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - move down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - move up
- `Return` - open message to see items
- `q` / `Escape` - back to main

**Detail View (subagent message items):**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - move down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - move up
- `Tab` / `Return` - toggle or drill deeper
- `e` - expand all
- `c` - collapse all
- `q` / `Escape` - back to message list

**Debug Viewer:**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - move down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - move up
- `q` / `Escape` - back to list

**Team Board:**
- `q` / `Escape` - back to list

**Session Picker:**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - navigate down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - navigate up
- `G` - jump to last
- `g` - jump to first
- `Return` - select session
- `q` - quit/exit
- `h` / `ArrowLeft` - focus sidebar

**Sidebar (all views):**
- `j` / `ArrowDown` - navigate down
- `k` / `ArrowUp` - navigate up
- `Return` - select project
- `l` / `ArrowRight` / `Escape` - unfocus sidebar
- `q` - quit/exit

---

### DISCREPANCIES FROM README:

#### 1. **Missing from README but implemented in code (Web Frontend):**
- `?` key - toggle keybind hints (exists in ALL views)
- `h` / `ArrowLeft` - focus sidebar (List view and Session picker view)
- `l` / `ArrowRight` - unfocus sidebar (Sidebar-focused mode)
- `h` / `l` / `ArrowLeft` / `ArrowRight` - switch focused columns (Detail view)
- Arrow key variants (`ArrowUp`, `ArrowDown`, `ArrowLeft`, `ArrowRight`) are fully supported alongside vim keys

#### 2. **Missing from README but implemented in TUI:**
- No "?" keybinding for toggling hints in TUI (not implemented)
- `Tab` key uses keyboard.tab, not `key.return` for detail view items
- Sidebar navigation with `h`/`l` keys
- Jump to top/bottom in Session Picker (`G`/`g`)
- `h`/`ArrowLeft` to focus sidebar in Session Picker

#### 3. **README states Session Picker has "q/Esc" to go back to list:**
- Web Frontend: This is correct when returning from a loaded session. But when in picker view initially (no session loaded), these take you to a list view (not back).
- TUI: `q` exits/quits the application from Session Picker, not back to list

#### 4. **Key behavior differences between Web and TUI not mentioned in README:**
- Web uses session "picker" state (separate view), TUI also has picker view
- Web has detail view with multi-column navigation (h/l), TUI has simpler detail view
- Web has explicit sidebar with project tree navigation
- TUI has similar sidebar navigation but integrated differently

#### 5. **Missing Tab keybinding clarification in README:**
- The README says `Tab` toggles "expand/collapse current message" in List view, but the web frontend also supports `Tab` in Detail view for items, and TUI has different Tab behavior in detail view

---

### SUMMARY OF DISCREPANCIES:

**Critical Issues:**
1. The `?` key for toggling keybind hints is NOT documented in README but is implemented across all views in web frontend
2. Sidebar navigation keys (`h`/`l`/`ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight`) are NOT documented
3. Detail view column navigation keys (`h`/`l`) are NOT documented
4. Arrow key alternatives are NOT documented (only vim keys are mentioned)

**Medium Issues:**
1. TUI's `q` in Session Picker exits app (not "back to list") - misleading from README
2. Tab behavior differs between views (not clearly documented)
3. Jump commands `G`/`g` work in Session Picker but not documented

**Consistency Issues:**
- README treats Web and TUI keybindings as one section without distinguishing between them
- Some keybindings are view-specific but documented globally

# Claude Code Trace A desktop + web viewer for Claude Code session JSONL files. Built with [Tauri v2](https://v2.tauri.app/) (Rust backend + React frontend). Reads session logs from `~/.claude/` and renders them as a scrollable conversation with expandable tool calls, token counts, and live tailing. Works as a **native desktop app** (macOS, Linux, Windows) or as a **web app** in any browser. Inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude).

## Install ### cargo install (any platform with Rust + Node.js) ```bash git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git cd claude-code-trace ./script/install.sh # builds everything + installs to PATH claude-code-trace # desktop app claude-code-trace --web # web mode (opens browser) cctrace-tui # terminal UI (requires backend running) ``` ### Download pre-built Grab the latest release from [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases): | Platform | File | | -------- | ------------------------------- | | macOS | `.dmg` | | Linux | `.deb`, `.rpm`, `.AppImage` | | Windows | `.msi`, `.exe` (NSIS installer) | > [!IMPORTANT] > **macOS:** The app is unsigned. After installing, remove the quarantine attribute: > > ```bash > xattr -cr /Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app > ``` ### Run from source (no install) ```bash git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git cd claude-code-trace npm install npm run tauri dev -- -- --web # opens browser automatically ``` ## Requirements - [Rust](https://rustup.rs/) 1.77+ - Node.js 18+ - macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools (`xcode-select --install`) - Linux: `libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libayatana-appindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev libxdo-dev libssl-dev` - Windows: [WebView2](https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/) (pre-installed on Windows 10/11) ## Usage ### Desktop Launch the app to open the session picker. It auto-discovers all sessions from `~/.claude/projects/`. Click **Open in Browser** in the toolbar to switch to browser mode — this opens `http://localhost:1420` in your default browser and hides the desktop window. ### Web (standalone) If you installed the desktop app: ```bash # macOS /Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web # Or add a shell alias: alias cctrace='/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web' cctrace ``` If running from source: ```bash npm run tauri dev -- -- --web ``` Then open **http://localhost:1420** in any browser. Select a session to view the conversation. Click messages to expand tool calls, or open the detail view for full inspection. MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool calls are automatically detected and displayed with human-friendly names. For example, `mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot` renders as **MCP chrome-devtools** with the summary "take screenshot". Supported MCP servers include chrome-devtools, figma, atlassian, buildkite, cloudflare, and any other server following the `mcp____` naming convention. ### Keybindings `?` toggles keybind hints in any view. **List view** | Key | Action | | ----------------- | --------------------------------------- | | `j` / `k` | Move cursor down / up | | `G` / `g` | Jump to last / first message | | `Tab` | Toggle expand/collapse current message | | `e` / `c` | Expand / collapse all Claude messages | | `Enter` | Open detail view | | `d` | Open debug log viewer | | `t` | Open team task board (when teams exist) | | `s` / `q` / `Esc` | Open session picker | **Detail view** | Key | Action | | ----------- | ------------ | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | **Session picker** | Key | Action | | ----------- | --------------------- | | `j` / `k` | Navigate sessions | | `Enter` | Open selected session | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | **Debug log viewer** | Key | Action | | ----------- | ------------ | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | ## TUI (Terminal UI) A terminal-based viewer that connects to the same HTTP API. ## Install ### cargo install (any platform with Rust + Node.js) ```bash git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git cd claude-code-trace ./script/install.sh # builds everything + installs to PATH claude-code-trace # desktop app claude-code-trace --web # web mode (opens browser) cctrace-tui # terminal UI (requires backend running) ``` ### Download pre-built Grab the latest release from [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases): | Platform | File | | -------- | ------------------------------- | | macOS | `.dmg` | | Linux | `.deb`, `.rpm`, `.AppImage` | | Windows | `.msi`, `.exe` (NSIS installer) | > [!IMPORTANT] > **macOS:** The app is unsigned. #!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Install claude-code-trace binary + TUI.
# Builds the frontend, installs the Rust binary to ~/.cargo/bin,
# and links the TUI as a global npm command.

cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."

echo "==> Installing npm dependencies..."
npm install

echo "==> Building frontend..."
npm run build

echo "==> Installing binary via cargo..."
cargo install --path src-tauri

echo "==> Building TUI..."
cd tui
npm install
npm run build
npm link
cd ..

echo ""
echo "Installed! Run:"
echo "  claude-code-trace        # desktop app"
echo "  claude-code-trace --web  # web mode (opens browser)"
echo "  cctrace-tui              # terminal UI (requires backend running)"

>

> ```

```bash
git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git
cd claude-code-trace
npm install
npm run tauri dev -- -- --web   # opens browser automatically
```

## Requirements

- [Rust](https://rustup.rs/) 1.77+
- Node.js 18+
- macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools (`xcode-select --install`)

## Usage

### Desktop

Launch the app to open the session picker. It auto-discovers all sessions from `~/.claude/projects/`.

Click **Open in Browser** in the toolbar to switch to browser mode — this opens `http://localhost:1420` in your default browser and hides the desktop window.

### Web (standalone)

If you installed the desktop app:

```bash
# macOS
/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web

# Or add a shell alias:
alias cctrace='/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web'
cctrace
```

If running from source:

```bash
npm run tauri dev -- -- --web
```

Then open **http://localhost:1420** in any browser.

Select a session to view the conversation. Click messages to expand tool calls, or open the detail view for full inspection.

# Claude Code Trace A desktop + web viewer for Claude Code session JSONL files. Built with [Tauri v2](https://v2.tauri.app/) (Rust backend + React frontend). Reads session logs from `~/.claude/` and renders them as a scrollable conversation with expandable tool calls, token counts, and live tailing. Works as a **native desktop app** (macOS, Linux, Windows) or as a **web app** in any browser. Inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude).

## Install ### cargo install (any platform with Rust + Node.js) ```bash git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git cd claude-code-trace ./script/install.sh # builds everything + installs to PATH claude-code-trace # desktop app claude-code-trace --web # web mode (opens browser) cctrace-tui # terminal UI (requires backend running) ``` ### Download pre-built Grab the latest release from [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases): | Platform | File | | -------- | ------------------------------- | | macOS | `.dmg` | | Linux | `.deb`, `.rpm`, `.AppImage` | | Windows | `.msi`, `.exe` (NSIS installer) | > [!IMPORTANT] > **macOS:** The app is unsigned. After installing, remove the quarantine attribute: > > ```bash > xattr -cr /Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app > ``` ### Run from source (no install) ```bash git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git cd claude-code-trace npm install npm run tauri dev -- -- --web # opens browser automatically ``` ## Requirements - [Rust](https://rustup.rs/) 1.77+ - Node.js 18+ - macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools (`xcode-select --install`) - Linux: `libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libayatana-appindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev libxdo-dev libssl-dev` - Windows: [WebView2](https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/) (pre-installed on Windows 10/11) ## Usage ### Desktop Launch the app to open the session picker. It auto-discovers all sessions from `~/.claude/projects/`. Click **Open in Browser** in the toolbar to switch to browser mode — this opens `http://localhost:1420` in your default browser and hides the desktop window. ### Web (standalone) If you installed the desktop app: ```bash # macOS /Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web # Or add a shell alias: alias cctrace='/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web' cctrace ``` If running from source: ```bash npm run tauri dev -- -- --web ``` Then open **http://localhost:1420** in any browser. Select a session to view the conversation. Click messages to expand tool calls, or open the detail view for full inspection. MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool calls are automatically detected and displayed with human-friendly names. For example, `mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot` renders as **MCP chrome-devtools** with the summary "take screenshot". Supported MCP servers include chrome-devtools, figma, atlassian, buildkite, cloudflare, and any other server following the `mcp____` naming convention. ### Keybindings `?` toggles keybind hints in any view. **List view** | Key | Action | | ----------------- | --------------------------------------- | | `j` / `k` | Move cursor down / up | | `G` / `g` | Jump to last / first message | | `Tab` | Toggle expand/collapse current message | | `e` / `c` | Expand / collapse all Claude messages | | `Enter` | Open detail view | | `d` | Open debug log viewer | | `t` | Open team task board (when teams exist) | | `s` / `q` / `Esc` | Open session picker | **Detail view** | Key | Action | | ----------- | ------------------------------- | | `j` / `k` | Navigate items | | `Tab` | Toggle expand/collapse item | | `Enter` | Open subagent or toggle expand | | `h` / `l` | Switch panels left / right | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | **Session picker** | Key | Action | | ----------- | --------------------- | | `j` / `k` | Navigate sessions | | `Enter` | Open selected session | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | **Debug log viewer** | Key | Action | | ----------- | ------------ | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | ## TUI (Terminal UI) A terminal-based viewer that connects to the same HTTP API. No browser needed — just Node.js + a terminal. ```bash # First time setup cd tui && npm install && cd .. # Single command (starts backend + TUI together): npm run dev:tui # Or if you installed via script/install.sh: claude-code-trace --web & # start backend in background cctrace-tui # run TUI ``` Features: session picker, message list with expand/collapse, live tailing via SSE, vim-style keybinds (j/k/G/g/Tab/e/c/q). ## Development ```bash npm install npm run tauri dev # desktop app with hot reload npm run dev:web # web mode (opens browser, no desktop window) npm run dev:tui # TUI (starts backend + terminal UI together) npm run tauri build # production build ``` ### Check & Test ```bash npm run check # run all checks at once npx vitest run # frontend tests cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust tests npx tsc --noEmit # TypeScript type check npx oxlint # JS/TS lint npx oxfmt # JS/TS format cargo clippy --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust lint cargo fmt --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust format ``` ## Release Push a version tag to trigger a GitHub Actions build: ```bash git tag v0.4.0 git push origin v0.4.0 ``` This creates a draft release with macOS, Linux, and Windows artifacts attached. Review and publish it from the [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases) page. ## Attribution Parsing heuristics inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude) and [claude-devtools](https://github.com/matt1398/claude-devtools). ## License [MIT](LICENSE)

# Claude Code Trace A desktop + web viewer for Claude Code session JSONL files. Built with [Tauri v2](https://v2.tauri.app/) (Rust backend + React frontend). Reads session logs from `~/.claude/` and renders them as a scrollable conversation with expandable tool calls, token counts, and live tailing. Works as a **native desktop app** (macOS, Linux, Windows) or as a **web app** in any browser. Inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude).

## Install ### Build from source (any platform with Rust + Node.js) ```bash git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git cd claude-code-trace ./script/install.sh # builds everything + installs to PATH claude-code-trace # desktop app claude-code-trace --web # web mode (opens browser) cctrace-tui # terminal UI (requires backend running) ``` ### Download pre-built Grab the latest release from [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases): | Platform | File | | -------- | ------------------------------- | | macOS | `.dmg` | | Linux | `.deb`, `.rpm`, `.AppImage` | | Windows | `.msi`, `.exe` (NSIS installer) | > [!IMPORTANT] > **macOS:** The app is unsigned. After installing, remove the quarantine attribute: > > ```bash > xattr -cr /Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app > ``` ### Run from source (no install) ```bash git clone git@github.com:delexw/claude-code-trace.git cd claude-code-trace npm install npm run tauri dev # desktop app with hot reload npm run dev:web # web mode (opens browser) npm run dev:tui # TUI (starts backend + terminal UI) ``` ## Requirements - [Rust](https://rustup.rs/) 1.77+ - Node.js 18+ - macOS: Xcode Command Line Tools (`xcode-select --install`) - Linux: `libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libayatana-appindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev libxdo-dev libssl-dev` - Windows: [WebView2](https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/webview2/) (pre-installed on Windows 10/11) ## Usage ### Desktop Launch the app to open the session picker. It auto-discovers all sessions from `~/.claude/projects/`. Click **Open in Browser** in the toolbar to switch to browser mode — this opens `http://localhost:1420` in your default browser and hides the desktop window. ### Web (standalone) If you installed the desktop app: ```bash # macOS /Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web # Or add a shell alias: alias cctrace='/Applications/Claude\ Code\ Trace.app/Contents/MacOS/Claude\ Code\ Trace --web' cctrace ``` If running from source: ```bash npm run dev:web ``` Then open **http://localhost:1420** in any browser. Select a session to view the conversation. Click messages to expand tool calls, or open the detail view for full inspection. MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool calls are automatically detected and displayed with human-friendly names. For example, `mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot` renders as **MCP chrome-devtools** with the summary "take screenshot". Supported MCP servers include chrome-devtools, figma, atlassian, buildkite, cloudflare, and any other server following the `mcp____` naming convention. ### Keybindings `?` toggles keybind hints in any view. **List view** | Key | Action | | ----------------- | --------------------------------------- | | `j` / `k` | Move cursor down / up | | `G` / `g` | Jump to last / first message | | `Tab` | Toggle expand/collapse current message | | `e` / `c` | Expand / collapse all Claude messages | | `Enter` | Open detail view | | `d` | Open debug log viewer | | `t` | Open team task board (when teams exist) | | `s` / `q` / `Esc` | Open session picker | **Detail view** | Key | Action | | ----------- | ------------------------------- | | `j` / `k` | Navigate items | | `Tab` | Toggle expand/collapse item | | `Enter` | Open subagent or toggle expand | | `h` / `l` | Switch panels left / right | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | **Session picker** | Key | Action | | ----------- | --------------------- | | `j` / `k` | Navigate sessions | | `Enter` | Open selected session | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | **Debug log viewer** | Key | Action | | ----------- | ------------ | | `q` / `Esc` | Back to list | ## TUI (Terminal UI) A terminal-based viewer that connects to the same HTTP API. No browser needed — just Node.js + a terminal. ```bash # First time setup cd tui && npm install && cd .. # Single command (starts backend + TUI together): npm run dev:tui # Or if you installed via script/install.sh: claude-code-trace --web & # start backend in background cctrace-tui # run TUI ``` Features: session picker, message list with expand/collapse, live tailing via SSE, vim-style keybinds (j/k/G/g/Tab/e/c/q). ## Development ```bash npm install npm run tauri dev # desktop app with hot reload npm run dev:web # web mode (opens browser, no desktop window) npm run dev:tui # TUI (starts backend + terminal UI together) npm run tauri build # production build ``` ### Check & Test ```bash npm run check # run all checks at once npx vitest run # frontend tests cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust tests npx tsc --noEmit # TypeScript type check npx oxlint # JS/TS lint npx oxfmt # JS/TS format cargo clippy --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust lint cargo fmt --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml # Rust format ``` ## Release Push a version tag to trigger a GitHub Actions build: ```bash git tag v0.4.0 git push origin v0.4.0 ``` This creates a draft release with macOS, Linux, and Windows artifacts attached. Review and publish it from the [Releases](https://github.com/delexw/claude-code-trace/releases) page. ## Attribution Parsing heuristics inspired by [tail-claude](https://github.com/kylesnowschwartz/tail-claude) and [claude-devtools](https://github.com/matt1398/claude-devtools). ## License [MIT](LICENSE)

Here's a summary of all fixes made to README.md:

**Commands (start web, TUI, GUI):**
1. **"Run from source" section** — replaced single verbose `npm run tauri dev -- -- --web` with all three modes: `npm run tauri dev` (desktop), `npm run dev:web` (web), `npm run dev:tui` (TUI)
2. **"Web from source"** — replaced `npm run tauri dev -- -- --web` with `npm run dev:web`
3. **TUI section** — changed `claude-code-trace --web &` to `claude-code-trace --headless &` (avoids opening an unnecessary browser tab when you just need the API backend)
4. **Install section title** — renamed "cargo install" to "Build from source" (the actual command is `./script/install.sh`, not `cargo install` directly)

**Keybindings:**
5. **Detail view** — expanded from just `q/Esc` to include `j/k` (navigate items), `Tab` (toggle expand), `Enter` (open subagent), `h/l` (switch panels)
